w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Jason Company, a Philippine Corporation, sold inventory on credit to a Japanese Company on April 8, 2020. Jason received payment of 35,000 yens on May 8, 2020. The exchange rate was
on May 8. What amount of foreign exchange gain or loss should be recognized? (rounded to the nearest peso)
Jane had the following foreign currencies transactions during 2020:
Purchased merchandise from a foreign supplier on January 20, 2020, for the Philippine peso equivalent to
and paid the invoice on April 20, 2020, at the Philippine peso equivalent to
On September 1, 2020, borrowed the Philippine peso equivalent of
evidenced by a note that is payable in the lender’s local currency on September 1, 2021. On December 31, 2020, the Philippine peso equivalent of the principal amount of
In Jane’s 2020 income statement, what amount should be included is a foreign exchange loss?
Assuming that Joshua entered into no forward contract, how much foreign exchange gain or loss it reports on its 2020 income statement about this transaction?

Assuming that Joshua entered into a forward contract to sell 10 million foreign currencies on December 1, 2020, as a fair value of hedge of foreign currency receivable, what is the net impact on its net income in 2020 resulting from a fluctuation in the value of the foreign currencies?

Corporation subsidiary buys marketable equity securities and inventory on April 1, 2020, for 100,000 foreign currencies each. It pays for both items on June 1, 2020, and they are still on hand at year-end. Inventory is carried at a cost under the lower-of-cost-or market value. Currency exchange rates for 1 peso follow:
Assume that the foreign currency is the subsidiary’s functional currency. What balances does a consolidated balance sheet report as of December 31, 2020?

Aly Corporation subsidiary buys marketable equity securities and inventory on April 1, 2020, for 100,000 foreign currencies each. It pays for both items on June 1, 2020, and they are still on hand at year-end. Inventory is carried at a cost under the lower-of-cost-or market value. Currency exchange rates for 1 peso follow:
Assume that the peso is the subsidiary’s functional currency. What balances does a consolidated balance sheet report as of December 31, 2020?
On January 1, 2020, Leonardo Co. sold equipment to its subsidiary, Bernie Corp., for
. The equipment had cost P 125,000, and the balance in accumulated depreciation was

. The equipment has estimated remaining useful life of eight years and no salvage value. Both companies use straight-line depreciation. On their separate 2014 income statements, Leonardo and Bernie reported depreciation expense of
, respectively. The amount of depreciation expense on the consolidated income statement for 2020 would have been
What is the remeasurement gain to be recognized on the consolidated income statement?
Which of the following items is not remeasurement using historical exchange rate under the temporal or remeasurement method?

George Corporation starts a subsidiary in a foreign country; the subsidiary's functional currency is not the currency of a hyperinflationary economy (using the current rate method). On January 1, George buys all of the subsidiary's common stock for 20,000 foreign currencies (FC). On April 1, the subsidiary purchases inventory for 20,000 FCs with payment made on May 1, and sells this inventory on August 1, for 30,000 FCs, which it collects on October 1. Currency exchange rates for one foreign currency are as follows:
In preparing consolidated financial statements, what translation adjustment will George report at the end of the current year?

On April 1, Quality Corporation, a Philippine Company, expects to order merchandise from a foreign supplier in three-months, denominating the transaction in foreign currencies, On April 1, the exchange rate is P 1.19 per foreign currency and Quality enters into a three-month forward contract to purchase 400,000 foreign currencies at a rate of 1.20. At the end of three months, three months, the exchange rate is P 1.21 per euro and Quality orders and receives the merchandise, paying 400,000 foreign currencies. What are the effects on net income from these transactions?
negative Adjustment to Net income when merchandise is received
negative Adjustment to Net income when merchandise is received
negative Adjustment to Net income when
positive Adjustment to Net income when merchandise is received
positive Adjustment to Net income when merchandise is received

Karen Inc., a Philippine Company, expects to order goods from a foreign supplier for 100,000 foreign currencies, with delivery and payment to be made on April 17. On January 17, Karen purchased a three-month call option on 100,000 foreign currencies and designated this. Option as a cash flow hedge of a forecasted foreign currency transaction. The following exchange rates apply:
What amount will Karen include as an option expense in net income during the period January 17 to April 17?

Company purchases raw material from its foreign supplier, Bolivian Clay, on May 8. Payment of 2,000,000 foreign currency- units (FC) is due in 30 days. May 31 is Brisco’s fiscal year-end. The pertinent exchange rates were as follows:
How much foreign exchange gain or loss should Brisco record on May 31?

Jane, Inc., a Philippine Company, has a Japanese yen account receivable resulting from an export sale on March 1 to a customer in Japan. The exporter signed a forward contract on March 1 to sell yen and designated it’s as a cash flow hedge of a recognized receivable. The exchange rate was
. Which of the following did the Philippines exporter report in net income?
The entries on December 31, 2020, include a
Credit to Foreign Currency Payable to Exchange Broker,
Debit to Foreign Currency Receivable from Exchange Broker,
Debit to Foreign Currency Receivable from Exchange Broker,
The entries on January 30, 2021, include a
Debit a Pesos Payable to Exchange Broker,
Credit to Premium on Forward Contract,
Credit to Foreign Currency Receivable from Exchange Broker,
The entries on January 30, 2021, include a
Debit to Foreign Currency transaction Loss,
Debit to Pesos Payable to Exchange Broker,
The entries on January 30, 2020, include a
Debit to Pesos Payable to Exchange Broker,
Credit to foreign Currency Transaction Gain,
Credit to Foreign Currency Receivable from Exchange Broker,
The present value of the average excess earnings over the next four years discounted at 15%. (The present value of an ordinary annuity of I at I 5% for four periods is 2.85498)
The capitalization of the average excess earnings of Sayo Na Co. at 18%.
The purchase of average excess earnings over the next four years.
The capitalization of average excess earnings of Sayo Na Co. at 24%.
to purchase the assets and assume the liabilities of Sayo Na Co., how much would be charged to goodwill?
Goodwill is equal to the capitalization of excess earnings at 15%.
Goodwill is equal to 3 years of excess earnings.
Goodwill is equal to the present value of excess earnings discounted at 15% for three years. (The present value factor of an ordinary annuity of 1 at 15% for three periods is 2.28323.)
A company is considering purchasing another company, which has the following assets and liabilities.
If the purchase price is 12,600,000, the amount of goodwill to be charged in recording the acquisition is
On April 1, 2020, AV Corp. acquired 80% of the outstanding stocks of SR Corp. for
. SR Corp.'s stockholders' equity at the end of 2020 is as follows: Common stock,
. The fair value of the non- controlling interest is
. All the assets of SR were fairly valued except for its inventories, which are undervalued by
, and the Patent, which is undervalued by

. The said patent has a remaining useful life of five years. Both companies use the straight-line method for depreciation and amortization. Shareholders' equity of AV Corp. on December 31, 2020, is composed of Common stock,
. Goodwill, if any, should be decreased by
every year-end. No additional issuance of capital stocks occurred.
The Antony Company acquired a 60% interest in the Jeano Company when Jeano's equity comprised share capital of
. Jeano's current statement of financial position shows share capital of
Consolidated and separate financial statements, what figure in respect of the noncontrolling interest should be included in Antony's consolidated statement of financial position?
On October 1, 2020, The Regan Company acquired 100% of the Elijah Company when the fair value of Elijah's net assets was
million, and its carrying amount was
million in cash transferred at the acquisition date, plus another

million in cash to be transferred 11 months after the acquisition date, if a specified profit target was met by Elijah. At the acquisition date, there was only a low probability of the profit target being met, so the fair value of the additional consideration liability was
million. In the event, the profit target was met, and the
What amount should Regan present for goodwill in its statement of consolidated financial position at December 31, 2021, according to
100% of the equity share capital of the Richmond Company was acquired by The Janice Company on June 30, 2020. Janice issued 500,000 new
ordinary shares, which had a fair value of
of each at the acquisition date. Also, the acquisition resulted in Janice incurring fees payable to external advisers of
3 Business Combinations, goodwill at the acquisition gate is measured by subtracting the identifiable assets acquired and the liabilities assumed from:
Jason Co. acquired inventories on May 1, 2020, from its 70% owned subsidiary, Paz Company. The inventories were sold for
, including the 25% markup on cost. Out of these inventories, 65% were sold to outsiders. During the year, Jason reported a net income of
, and Paz reported a net income of P 140,000.
How much is the realized profit to be allocated to non controlling interest in 2021?
The Sylvia Company acquired a 70% interest in The Clarke Company for
when the fair value of Clarke's identifiable assets and liabilities was
. Sylvia acquired a 65% interest in The Homer Company for
when the fair value of Homer's identifiable assets and liabilities was

. Sylvia measures non-controlling interest at the relevant share if the identifiable net assets at the acquisition date. Neither Clarke nor Homer has any contingent liabilities at the amounts in their financial statements. Annual impairment reviews have not resulted in any impairment losses being recognized.
3 Business Combinations, what is the goodwill that should be included in Sylvia's consolidated statement of financial position?
The Brown Company acquired 80% of the Ayannah Company for a consideration transferred to
million. The consideration was estimated to include a control premium of
million at the acquisition date. Are the following statements true or false, according to
million if the non-controlling interest is measured at its
million if the non-controlling interest is measured at fair value?
The working paper elimination entry recorded by Parent Company on January 1, 2020, the date of acquisition of the Subsidiary Company follows:
What is the control premium paid by the parent company under the business combination?

Patrick Company acquired the assets (except for cash) and assumed the liabilities of Steve Company on January 2, 2020, and Steve Company is dissolved. As compensation, Patrick Company gave 24,000 shares of its common stock, 12,000 shares of its 8% preferred stock, and cash of
to the stockholders of Steve Company. On the date of acquisition, Patrick Company had the following characteristics:
Immediately before the acquisition, Steve Company's balance sheet was as follows:
An appraisal of Steve Company showed that the fair values of its assets and liabilities were equal to their book values except for the following, which had fair values as indicated:
How much must be the goodwill recognized as a result of this business combination?
On October 1, 2013, The Fernando Company acquired I 00% of The Austria
when the fair value of Austria's net assets was
million, and their carrying amount was
million in cash transferred at the acquisition date, plus another

million in cash to be transferred 11 months after the acquisition date if a specified profit target was met by Austria. At the acquisition date, there was only a low probability of the profit target being met, so the fair value of the additional consideration liability was P 10 million. In the event, the profit target was met, and the
million cash was transferred. What amount should Fernando present for goodwill in its statement of consolidated financial position at December 31, 2020, according to
PSY Corporation owns 90%, of the outstanding common shares of SVG Company. On January 2, 2020, office equipment that had a carrying value to SVG Company
and has a remaining life of 10 years was sold to PSY Corporation for
August 31, 2021, PSY Corporation sole a second-hand delivery van to
Included in the January 1, 2021 inventory of PSY Company was merchandise inventory worth

on its December 31, 2021. These inventories came from inter-company sales and purchases. PSY Corporation included a mark-up of 25% on cost while SVG Company charged a 30% mark-up on sales. Each of the two companies has net incomes in 2020 and 2021 as follows:
What is the amount of the consolidated net income attributable to controlling interest in 2021?
Elizabeth Company acquired 100% of Louie Inc. on January 5, 2020. During 2020, Elizabeth sold Louie for
. Louie still owned 40% of the goods at the end of the year. The cost of goods sold was
for Louie. What was the consolidated cost of goods sold?
Michael Corporation owned 70% of the voting common stock of Gabriel Company. On January 2, 2020, Gabriel sold a parcel of land to Michael. The land had a book value of
and was sold to Michael for
. Gabriel's reported net income for 2020 was 119,000. What is the non-controlling interest's share of Gabriel's net income?
Company owned all of the voting common stock of Jorge Co. On January 2, 2020,
sold some equipment to Jorge for
. At the time of the sale, the balance in accumulated depreciation was
remaining life of five years and a P-0- salvage value, Straight-line depreciation is used by both
and Jorge. At what amount should the equipment (net depreciation) be included on the Consolidated balance sheet dated December 31, 2020?
The Joanne Company owns 65% of The Mary Company. On December 31, 2020, the last day of the accounting period, Joanne sold Mary a noncurrent asset for
, and on December 31, 2020, Its carrying amount in Joanne's book was
. The group's consolidated statement of financial position has been drafted without any adjustments about this non-current asset.

Under PAS 27 Consolidated and Separate Financial Statements, what adjustments should be made to the consolidated statement of financial position figures for non-current assets and non-controlling interest?
Non-current assets (increase by P 1,500); Non-controlling Interest (increase by
Non-current assets (increase by P 1,500); Non-controlling Interest (reduce by
Non-current assets (increase by P 1,500); Non-controlling Interest (no change)
The Conrad Company acquired equipment on January 1, 2020, at the cost of
, depreciating it over eight years with a nil residual value. On January 1, 2023, The Cris Company acquired 100% of Conrad and estimated the fair value of the equipment at
, with a remaining life of 5 years. This fair value was not incorporated into Conrad's book, and depreciation expense continued to be calculated by reference to the original cost.

Under PAS 27 Consolidated and Separate Financial Statements, what adjustments should be made to the depreciation expense for the year and the statement of financial position carrying amount in preparing the consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2024?
Depreciation expense (increase by ?8,000); Carrying Amount (decrease QY
The Josh Company acquired an 80% interest in The Earl Company when Earl's equity comprised share capital of
. Earl's current statement of financial position shows share capital of P 100,000, a revaluation reserve of
, and retained earnings o f P 1,4 00,000 .
Under PAS 27 Consolidated and separate financial statements, what figure in respect of Earl's retained earnings should be included in the consolidated statement of financial position?
The Kareen Company owns 75% of The Goldy Company. The following figures are from their separate financial statement:
Kareen Trade receivable P 1,040,000, including P 30,000 due from Goldy.
Goldy Trade receivable P 215,000, including P 40,000 due from Kareen.
Under PAS 27 Consolidated and separate financial statements, what figure should appear for trade receivables in Kareen's consolidated statement of financial position?
Cesar Company holds 90% of the common stock of Diane Company During 2020, Cesar reported sales of

and cost of goods sold of P 840,000. For this same period, Diane has sales of P 420,000 and cost of goods sold of P 252,000. Also, during 2020, Cesar sold merchandise to Diane for P 140,000. The subsidiary still possesses 40% of this inventory at the end of 2020.
What is the consolidated cost of goods sold?
Assuming that the transfers were from Diane Co. to Cesar Co., what is the consolidated cost of goods sold?
Assuming that the transfers were from Diane Co. to Cesar Co. what are the consolidated sales?

Baste Company owns an 80% controlling interest in the Bastion Company. Bastion regularly sells merchandise to Baste, which then sold to outside parties. The gross profit on all such sales is 40%. On .January I. 2020, Baste sold land and a building to Bastion. The value of the parcel is 20% to land and 80% to structures. The data are the following:
Cost of real estate sold on January 1, 2020
The sales price of real estate on January 1,
For 2020, what is the consolidated comprehensive income attributable to controlling interest?
Corporation Lizzy acquired 2,000 shares of the voting stock of Corporation Lizette in the open market at
per share. Direct costs associated with the acquisition total of
. Balance sheets of both companies on January 1, 2020, immediately after the acquisition of shares of Lizzy are as follows:
The fair values of Lizzy and Lizette's assets on January 1, 2020, are presented below. Liabilities of both companies are properly valued at their respective book value:
The total consolidated assets must be
Victor Corporation owns 80 percent of the stock of Vicky Company. At the end of 2020, Victor Corporation and Vicky Company reported the following partial operating results and inventory balances:

Victor Corporation regularly prices its products at cost plus a 40 percent markup for profit. Vicky Company prices its sales at cost plus a 20 percent markup. The total sales reported by Victor and Vicky include both intercompany sales and sales to non-affiliates.
The controlling interest net income for 2020 must be
Rommel, Inc. acquired a 60% interest in Mikee Company several years ago. During 2020, Mikee sold inventory costing
to Rommel for P 100,000. A total of 16% of this inventory was not sold to an outsider until 2021. During 2021, Mikee sold inventory costing
to Rommel for P 120,000. A total of 35% of this inventory was not sold to outsiders until 2022. In 2015, Rommel reported the cost of sales of
. What is the consolidated cost of sales
The following are information regarding parent and subsidiary.
,000 of this inventory on hand at year-end. Clark owns a 15% interest in Dean and does not exert significant influence.
from Kent Corporation, a wholly-owned subsidiary. Kent’s gross profit on sale was
of this inventory remaining on December 31, 2020
Before eliminating entries, Clark had consolidated current assets of
. What amount should Clark report in its December 31, 2020, consolidated balance sheet for current assets?
Par Company owns 60% of Sub Corp's outstanding capital stock. On May 1, 2020, Par advanced Sub
in cash, which was still outstanding at December 31, 2020. What
portion of this advance should be eliminated in the preparation of the December 31, 2020, consolidated balance sheet?
. At the time of the sale, the equipment had been 50% depreciated using the straight-line method and an assigned life of
continued depreciating the equipment by using the straight-line method over the remaining life of 5 years.
What is the amount of the intercompany profit or loss that must be deferred on 12/31/2020?
The Ezra Company acquired an 80% interest in the Elaine Company when Blaine's equity comprised share capital of
. Elaine's current statement of financial position shows share capital of
Under PAS 27, Consolidated and Separate Financial Statements, what figure in respect of Elaine's retained earnings should be included in the consolidated statement of financial position?
Dickie Corporation contracted to build a building for Dickson Company. The contract price was
, and Dickie estimated that construction costs would total

. The construction period lasted until September 1, 2020. Costs during each period, the estimated total cost of the product at the end of the year, billings and cash collected during the year were as follows:
The amount of gross profit recognized in 2021 using the percentage of completion method must be:
The following information pertains to the building contract of Carrie Construction Company, wherein the fixed contract price is

Assume that all costs are incurred, all billings to customers are made, and all collections from costumers are received within 30 days of billing, as planned. Under the percentage-of-completion method of revenue recognition is used, how much is the income from construction for the year 2022?

Celso Construction is in its fourth year of business. Celso performs long-term construction projects and accounts for them using the percentage of completion. Celso built an apartment building at a price of
. The costs and billings for this contract for the first three years are as follows:
Estimated costs yet to be incurred
Determine the income from construction in 2015.
In 2020, Jerico Construction Company was contracted to do a private road network of Philip Corporation for
million. The project was estimated to be completed in two years.
The construction contract provided the number of other things the following:
5% mobilization fee (to be deducted from the last billing) payable within 15 days after the signing of the contract.
Retention provision of 10% on all billings.
Progress billings on construction are payable within seven days from date of acceptance.

Jerico estimated its gross margin on the project at 25% and used the percentage of Completion method of accounting. By the end of the year, Jerico presented progress billings corresponding to 50% completion. Philip Corporation accepted all the bills presented except the last one for 10%, which was accepted on January 10. Except for the last billing of 8% accepted in 2020, which was due on January 3, 2022, all accepted billings were settled in 2020. The gross profit recognized by Jerico Construction Company for 2020 is:
The gross profit recognized by Jerico Construction Company for 2020 is:

A construction company began operations in 2020. Construction activity for the first year is shown below. All contracts are with different customers, and any work remaining on December 31, 2020, is expected to be completed in 2021.
Determine the income from construction to be reported in the income statement for the year 2020.
Construction Company contracted to build an office building for RH Corporation for a total contract price of

million. On July 1, Mean estimated that it would take between 2 to 3 years to complete the building. On December 31, 2022, the building was deemed substantially completed. The following are accumulated contract costs incurred, total estimated costs, and accumulated billings to RH for 2020, 2021, and 2022.

Using the percentage of completion method, determine the correct income (loss) from construction to be presented in the income statement of the company for the years 2020, 2021 and 2022, respectively.

has used the cost-to-cost percentage of completion method of recognizing profits. Tony assumed leadership of the business after the recent death of his father, Ton. In reviewing the records, Tony finds the following information regarding a recently completed building project for which the total contract price was
Construction in progress account balance 2020 P 10,000,000
How much cost was incurred in 2021?
Romeo Construction has consistently used the percentage of completion method. On January 10, 2020, Romeo began work on the
construction contract. At the inception date, the estimated cost of construction was
. The following data relate to the progress of the contract:
has incurred the following contract costs in the first year on a two-year fixed-price contract for
Other contract cost (including site labor costs)
How much profit or loss should
recognize in the first year of the three-year construction contract?
No profit or loss in the first year and deferring it to the second year.
Since 60% is the percentage of completion, recognize 60% of the loss.
In 2020, Mega world began construction work under a 3-year contract. The contract price was

. Mega world uses the percentage of completion method for financial accounting purposes. The income to be recognized each year is based on the proportion of costs incurred to total estimated costs for completing the contract. The financial statement presentation relating to this contract on December 31, 2020, were as follows:
Cost of uncompleted contract over billings
What was the initial estimated gross before tax on this contract?
contractors had a 3-year construction contract in 2020 for

. The company uses the percentage of completion method for financial statement purposes. Income to be recognized each year is based on the ratio of the cost incurred to the total estimated cost to complete the contract. Data on this contract follows:
Net income recognized in 2020 before tax
contractors maintain a separate bank account for each construction contract. Bank deposits to this contract amounted to
What was the estimated total income before the tax on this contract?
CC Corp. owns a subsidiary in Japan whose balance sheet in Japanese Yen for the last years follows:
Relevant exchange rates are (Yen to Peso):

CC formed the subsidiary on January 1, 2020. The income of the subsidiary was earned evenly throughout the years, and the subsidiary declared dividends worth ¥15,000 on September 12, 2020, and none were declared during 2021. How much is the cumulative translation adjustment for 2021?
The foreign exchange gain (loss) on option contract to be recognized in earnings on June 30
The foreign exchange gain (loss) on option contract to be recognized in equity on June 30
thin US territory wherein the functional currency is the US $. The presentation currency of the bank is Philippine peso. The following financial position data for the year 2020 are provided:
The following additional data are provided.
What is the translation gain (loss) to be recognized by BPI US in its Statement of Comprehensive Income for the year ended December 31, 2020?
On October 3, 2020, Mike Inc. entered into a forward contract with BPI Bank for the speculation to buy $100 to be delivered on January 30, 2021. The following direct exchange rates were provided:
What is the net foreign currency gain (loss) to be recognized by Mike for the years ended December 31, 2020, and December 31, 2021, respectively?
Hamon reported sales of 1,500,000 baht during 2014. What amount (rounded) would have been included for this subsidiary in calculating consolidated sales?
On December 31. Hamon had accounts receivable of 280,000 baht. What amount (rounded) would have been included for this subsidiary in calculating consolidated accounts receivable?

Paul Corporation, a Philippine Company, expects to order goods from a foreign supplier at 250,000 foreign currencies, with delivery and payment to be made on October 24. On July 24, Paul purchased a three-month call option for 250,000 foreign currencies and designated this option as a cash flow hedge of a forecasted foreign currency transaction. The following exchange rates apply.
What amount will Paul include as an option expense in net income during the period July 24 to October 24?
For what amount should Brisco's accounts payable be credited on May 8?
How much foreign currency will it cost Brisco to finally pay the payable on June 7?
Rikka Co. ordered parts costing 100,000 baht for a foreign supplier or May 12 when the exchange rate was
per baht. A one-month forward contract was signed on the date to purchase 100,000 baht at a forward rate of
per baht. On June 12, when the parts were received, and payment was made, the exchange rate was
per baht. At what amount should inventory be reported?
Patricia Company, a Philippine Corporation, sold inventory on December 1, 2020, with payment of 10,000 foreign currencies to be received in sixty days. The pertinent exchange rates were as follows:
For what amount should Sales be credited on December 1?

On December 1, 2020, Gary Inc. entered into a 120-day forward contract to purchase 250,000 US dollars for speculative purposes. Gary, Inc., fiscal year ends on December 31. The exchange rates are as follows:
How much is the forex gain or loss to be reported from this forward contract in 2021?
Purchased merchandise from a foreign supplier on January 20, 2020 for the Philippine peso equivalent of
and paid the invoice on April 20, 2020, at the Philippine peso equivalent of
On September 1, 2020, borrowed the Philippine peso equivalent of
evidence by a note that is payable in the lender's local currency on September 1, 2021. On December 31, 2020, the Philippine peso equivalent of the principal amount was
In Jane's income statement, what amount should be included as a foreign exchange loss?
The subsidiary’s functional currency is the local currency unit. What amount should Rose's balance sheet include for the preceding items?
The subsidiary's functional currency is peso. What amount should Rose’s balance sheet include for the preceding items?
Inc. (parent company) secured a foreign bank loan denominated in the functional currency when the spot rate was 1 Taiwan NT dollar =

. The bank loan has a principal amount of 200,000,000 NT dollars. Interest calculations are ignored. The bank loan is designated as a hedge of net investment and is considered to have satisfied all necessary criteria.
Selected exchange rates between the functional currency (NT dollar) and the peso are as follows:
The December 31, 2020 loans payable account balance obtained to hedge the net investment amounted to
On March 31, 2020, C received from X
representing franchise fee . Franchise services were immediately started by C, and these were completed on Oct. 31, 2020, at the cost of
. Further, the franchisor is entitled to a 5% fee on gross sales payable within the first ten days of the following month. The following year, the franchisee yielded gross sales of
. C's earned franchise fee for the year 2021:
On January 2, 2020: Magnolia Ice Cream signed an agreement authorizing Trisha to operate as a franchisee for an initial franchise fee

received upon signing of the agreement. Trisha commenced operations on August 1, 2020, at which date all of the initial services required of Magnolia Ice Cream had been performed at the cost of P 120,000. The franchise agreement further provides that Trisha must pay a 10% monthly continuing franchising fee. Sales reported from August 1 to December 31, 2020, amount to
What is the net income related to the franchise fee to be reported by Magnolia Ice Cream in 2020?
Ana's Inc. granted a franchise to
for the Makati area. The franchisee was to pay a franchisee of

, payable in five equal annual installments starting with the payment upon signing of the agreement. The franchise was to pay monthly 3% of gross sales of the preceding month. Should the operations of the outlet prove to be unprofitable, the franchise may be canceled with whatever obligations owing Ana's, Inc. in connection with the
franchise fee waived. The prevailing interest rate is 14%. The first year generated gross sales of
What is the amount of unearned franchise fee after the first year of operations?
Inc. signed an agreement to operate as a franchisee of Mang
for an initial franchise fee of
for ten years. Of this amount,
was paid when the agreement was signed and the balance payable in three annual payments beginning on Dec ember 31, 2020.
signed a non-interest bearing note for the balance. The implicit interest rate is 18%. Assume that substantial services amounting to
had already been rendered by the franchisor, and indirect costs of
If the collection of the note is not reasonably assured, calculate the net income. For the year ended December 31, 2020. Use PV factor 2.17.
On .January 2, 2020, Diversified Enterprises signed a franchise agreement with
Company for an initial franchise fee of
upon the signing of the franchise contract, and the balance is payable in four annual payments of
starting December 31, 2020. DTS I issued 12% interest-bearing notes for the balance. The collection of notes is not reasonably assured.

The down payment is not refundable; it represents the actual cost of initial services, provided by Diversified before formal signing. However, substantial additional services have yet to be performed by Diversified. During 2020 additional direct franchise costs of
will pay a continuing franchise fee at 3% of its gross sales revenue. The franchise outlet commenced business operations on October 1, 2020, and its gross sales totaled,
The net income recognized by Diversified from the
Kenneth Company, Inc. franchisor, entered into a franchise agreement with Orville Trading, franchisee, on March 31, 2020. The total franchise fee is

, of which P 100,000 is payable upon signing and the balance in four equal annual installments. The down payment is refundable in the event the franchisor fails to render services, and none thus far had been rendered. When Kenneth Company prepared its financial statements on March 31, 2020, the franchise fee revenue to be reported is:
Jodee Construction's Enterprises, a franchisor, charges franchisees a “franchise fee” of

is paid upon the signing of the contract with the balance payable in three equal installments after each year after that. Jodee Construction will assist in locating a suitable business site, conduct a market study, oversee the construction of facilities, and provides initial training for employees.

On December 1, 2020, Jodee Construction' signed a franchising agreement for the U-belt area. By the end of 2020, it was determined that the substantial performance of the initial services had cost Jodee Construction' a total
, and that collection of the balance of the franchise fee has been reasonably assured. In its 2020 income statement.
Jodee Construction’ should report franchise revenue and net income:
The Carl Company uses the installment method of reporting for accounting purposes. The roll owing data were obtained for the years 2020 to 2022:
In 2022, one of the customers defaulted in his payment, and the company repossessed the merchandise with an estimated market value of
. The sales were in 2020, and the unpaid balance on the date of the repossession was
Compute the total realized gross profit for 2021.
The Devant Appliances account for its sales on the installment basis. As the beginning of 2022 ledger accounts include the following balances:
At the end of 2022, account balances before adjustment for realized gross profit on installment sales are:

Installment sales in 2022 are made at 25% above the cost of merchandise sold. During 2022 upon default in payment by the customer, the company repossessed the merchandise with an estimated market value of
, and P 12,800 had been collected before repossession.
The realized gross profit on December 31, 2022 is
Cesar Company holds 90% of the common stock of Diane Company During 2020, Cesar reported sales of

and cost of goods sold of P 840,000. For this same period, Diane has sales of P 420,000 and cost of goods sold of P 252,000. Also, during 2020, Cesar sold merchandise to Diane for P 140,000. The subsidiary still possesses 40% of this inventory at the end of 2020.

The share of partners Michael in the 2020 corrected net income is:
The Home Office in Makati shipped merchandise costing
to the Manila branch and paid for the freight charges of
. The home office bills the branch at 125% of the cost. Manila branch was subsequently instructed to transfer one-half of the merchandise to Quezon City branch, wherein Quezon City branch paid for
freight. If the shipment were made directly from Makati to Quezon City, the freight cost would have been
. By how much will Manila branch charge the Home Office account?

merchandise to an agency to establish a working fund.

On January 5, 2021, the Glenda branch transferred half of the original shipments to the Sandy branch, and the Glenda branch pays
At what amounts should the 60% of the merchandise remaining unsold on December 31, 2020, be included in the inventory of the Glenda branch on December 31, 2020?

The Rommel Company was organized in 2015. Shortly after opening its doors to the public at the main store, Rommel Company established a branch in another city. At the end of the second year of operations, the home office received the following condensed income statement from the branch:
The management at the home office questioned the accuracy of these figures and assigned you the task of verifying the branch data. Your review of the records uncovered the following facts:
The beginning of year balance in unrealized profit to the branch was
During the period, the home office shipped goods to the branch that had cost the main store
. However, your review of the branch receiving reports revealed that several shipments from the home office had been recorded twice by the branch accountant.
The branch is billed a uniform 25% above cost and receives inventory only from the home office.
The branch ending inventory was correctly reported at a billed price of
When reconciling reciprocal accounts, you found that the branch had not recorded
of services performed by the Home Office and billed to the branch. All other selling and administrative expenses were correctly reported by the branch.
Compute the correct net income of the branch.

of furniture and fixtures failed to notify the home office. The bookkeeper knowing that all fixed assets are carried on the home office, recorded the proper entry on the branch office records. It is the Company’s policy not to take any depreciation assets acquired in the last half of a year.

Rainbow Products Corporation adopts perpetual inventory procedures. On installment sales, the corporation charges installment accounts receivable and credits inventory gross profit accounts.

and cost to dispose of them in the market at

in 2017. None of the contributions were spent on research in 2017. In 2020,
of the contributions were used to support the research activities of the society.

decrease in temporarily restricted net assets.
115. St. Luke's Hospital, a nonprofit hospital-affiliated, received the following cash contributions from donors during the year ended December 31, 2020:
Contributions restricted by donors for research
Contributions restricted by donors for capital acquisitions
Neither of the contributions was spent during 2020; however, during 2021, the hospital spent the entire
contribution on research and the entire

contribution on a capital asset, which was placed into service during the year. On the hospital's statement of Operations for the year ended December 31, 2021, what total amount should be reported for “net assets released from restrictions.”
TD decided to withdraw from his partnership with SM and MR. Before his withdrawal, TD's capital balance was P 58,000, while SM's was

. Also, the partnership's total assets amounted to P 450,000, but the partners agreed that a fixed asset was under depreciated by P 15,000. TD, SM, and MR share profits and losses in the ratio of 2:4:4, respectively, If TD was paid
upon his retirement, how much is the remaining partnership net assets after TD's withdrawal?

, how much was invested by Jet?

,000, respectively. The agreement further provides that C shall receive a minimum of
per annum and D a minimum of
which is inclusive of amounts representing interest and their respective shares in partnership profits. The balance of the profits shall be distributed among A, B, C, and D in the ratio of 3:3:2:2.

197. On September 1, 2020, Bain Company received an order for equipment from a foreign customer for 300,000 local
units (LCU) when the US dollar equivalent was

. Bain shipped the equipment on October 15, 2018, and billed the customer for 300,000 LCU when the US dollar equivalent was P 100,000. Bain received the customer remittance in full on November 16, 2018, and sold the 300,000 LCU for P 105,000. In the income statement for the year ended December 31, 2018, what amount should Bain report as part of net income a foreign exchange transaction gain?

per pula. On December 31 2020 Cano's yearend, the spot rate was
by February 1, 2021, when payment was received. How much should
report as foreign exchange transaction gain or loss as part of 2021 income?

, respectively. Interest on the note is 10% per annum.

paid Blyton his 2021 royalties on January 10, 2022.
2021 financial statements were issued on February 1, 2022. Spot rates for Canadian dollars were as follows:
accrue for royalties payable on December 31, 2021?

On December 1, 2022, an entity sold on account the said goods to a foreign customer at a selling price of P 1,500. The accounts receivable are collected on February 28, 2023.
The entity is operating in the Philippine economy wherein the functional currency is the Philippine Peso.

213. On December 1, 2020, Winston Company entered into a forward contract to purchase P 10,000 in 90 days to hedge a commitment to purchase equipment being manufactured to the entity's specifications.
